skill.name: ai-video-generation
skill.description: Generate AI videos with Google Veo, Seedance, Wan, Grok and 40+ models via inference.sh CLI. Models: Veo 3.1, Veo 3, Seedance 1.5 Pro, Wan 2.5, Grok Imagine Video, OmniHuman, Fabric, HunyuanVideo. Capabilities: text-to-video, image-to-video, lipsync, avatar animation, video upscaling, foley sound. Use for: social media videos, marketing content, explainer videos, product demos, AI avatars.
skill.summary: Generate AI videos with 40+ models including Google Veo, Seedance, Wan, and Grok via inference.sh CLI
skill.author: skillssh

security_audit.summary: All 66 detected patterns are false positives from documentation code examples in SKILL.md. The file contains only Markdown documentation with bash code blocks showing users how to use the external inference.sh CLI tool. No executable code, command injection vectors, or active network requests exist in this skill.

content.value_statement: Create professional AI-generated videos without managing multiple subscriptions or APIs. This skill provides unified access to 40+ video generation models through a single CLI interface for text-to-video, image animation, avatar creation, and video enhancement.
actual_capabilities: Generate videos from text prompts using 40+ AI models including Google Veo, Seedance, Wan, and Grok
Animate static images with image-to-video models like Wan 2.5 and P-Video
Create talking avatars with lipsync using OmniHuman, Fabric, and PixVerse
Upscale video quality with Topaz video upscaler
Add sound effects to videos using HunyuanVideo Foley
limitations: Requires installation of external inference.sh CLI tool
Dependent on third-party API availability and rate limits
Video generation speed varies by model and complexity
Some models have resolution or duration restrictions

use_cases.title: Social Media Content Creation
use_cases.description: Generate engaging video content for social platforms from text descriptions or product images
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content creators

use_cases.title: Marketing Video Production
use_cases.description: Create product demos, explainer videos, and marketing materials with AI-generated visuals
use_cases.target_user: Marketing professionals and small business owners

use_cases.title: Avatar and Animation Workflows
use_cases.description: Build automated pipelines for creating talking head videos and animating static images

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text-to-Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 5-second video showing {description}. Use the fastest available model with audio support.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick video generation from simple text descriptions

prompt_templates.title: Product Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate this product image: {image_url}. Create a {duration} second video showing the product from multiple angles with smooth camera movement.
prompt_templates.scenario: Converting product photos into dynamic videos

prompt_templates.title: Avatar Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a talking avatar using this portrait: {image_url} and this narration: {audio_url}. Use the best lipsync model available.
prompt_templates.scenario: Generating AI avatars with synchronized speech

prompt_templates.title: Video Enhancement Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a video at {video_url}. First upscale it to 4K quality, then add appropriate sound effects for {scene_description}. Finally, merge it with this second video: {second_video_url}.

output_examples.input: Generate a video of a drone shot flying over a forest at sunset
output_examples.output: Video generation initiated with model google/veo-3-1-fast. Your video will be ready in approximately 2-3 minutes. The output will include scenic aerial footage with warm golden-hour lighting and forest canopy textures.

output_examples.input: Animate this product image with a 360-degree rotation effect
output_examples.output: Image-to-video job submitted to falai/wan-2-5. Expected completion: 90 seconds. The output video will smoothly rotate your product image to showcase all angles.

output_examples.input: Create a talking avatar from this headshot with the attached narration
output_examples.output: Avatar generation started with bytedance/omnihuman-1-5. Processing time: approximately 4 minutes. The output will feature lip-synced animation matching your audio narration.

best_practices: Start with faster models like google/veo-3-1-fast for prototyping, then use higher-quality models for final renders
Use descriptive prompts with camera movements, lighting details, and scene composition for better results
For image-to-video, ensure source images are high resolution and have clear subjects for best animation quality
Batch similar video generation tasks to optimize API usage and manage rate limits effectively
anti_patterns: Do not generate videos from copyrighted material or trademarked characters without permission
Avoid using extremely long or complex prompts that may confuse the model or increase processing time
Do not attempt to generate deepfakes of real people without consent - this violates ethical guidelines
Avoid submitting multiple simultaneous requests to the same model which may trigger rate limiting

faq.question: Do I need to pay for each video generation model?
faq.answer: The skill itself is free, but each video generation model may have separate pricing through the inference.sh platform. Check the inference.sh pricing page for specific model costs.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without installing the inference.sh CLI?
faq.answer: No, this skill requires the inference.sh CLI tool to be installed. The skill provides documentation and examples for using the CLI, but cannot function without it.

faq.question: What is the maximum video length I can generate?
faq.answer: Maximum length varies by model. Most models generate 4-5 second videos by default. Some models like Grok support configurable durations up to 10 seconds. Check individual model documentation for specifics.

faq.question: Can I generate videos with custom branding or watermarks?
faq.answer: The models generate base videos without watermarks. You can add branding using post-processing tools or the media merger utility to combine videos with overlay elements.

faq.question: Are the generated videos copyrighted?
faq.answer: You generally own the rights to videos you generate. However, review the specific model's terms of use on inference.sh as rights may vary by provider.

faq.question: How long does video generation take?
faq.answer: Processing time varies by model complexity and resolution. Fast models like Veo 3.1 Fast typically complete in 1-2 minutes, while higher-quality models may take 4-6 minutes.
